meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Solve Debugging Bottlenecks with AI Workflows
content.seo_description: Master professional debugging workflows for freelancers. Overcome 'can't debug code' syndrome using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By integrating Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for iterative execution, you transform debugging from a manual slog into a streamlined, automated validation loop. This workflow isolates errors, identifies root causes, and generates production-ready patches in minutes, keeping your client deliverables on schedule.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting, and your terminal is bleeding red errors that defy logic. You are stuck in a loop of 'fix one, break two,' watching your profit margins vanish with every hour spent chasing phantom bugs. It is the silent killer of the freelance career—the moment your technical confidence hits a brick wall.

But the best builders don't suffer through manual debugging; they architect their way out of it. By shifting your workflow to include AI-driven diagnostic tools, you stop being a human debugger and start being a system orchestrator. It’s time to move from frantic trial-and-error to systematic, AI-accelerated resolution.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Domain
steps.description: Before fixing, define the boundary. Use Antigravity to map the module dependencies and identify exactly where the state corruption occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current execution flow and identify the specific function boundary where the stack trace originates. Map the dependencies and highlight variables that fail the schema validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze current directory and identify the source of the crash in the stack trace'

steps.title: Instrument for Observability
steps.description: Inject dynamic logging into the suspected modules to capture runtime state without cluttering your main codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a non-intrusive logging wrapper for the identified module that captures input/output state and variable mutations during runtime.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add structured logging to the target file to trace the variable values before the crash'

steps.title: Automate Reproduction
steps.description: Create a minimal reproduction script to ensure the fix is verifiable and repeatable, preventing regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standalone test script that reproduces the exact failure condition found in the stack trace using mocked data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a test file that reproduces the bug identified in step 1'

steps.title: AI-Driven Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Feed the reproduction logs into Claude Code to get a technical diagnosis of the logical flaw.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the reproduction logs and the source code. Explain the logical inconsistency causing the runtime error in plain technical terms.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze the error logs and provide a fix for the identified root cause'

steps.title: Validate and Deploy
steps.description: Apply the fix, run the test suite, and verify the client requirement is met without side effects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Verify the proposed fix against the reproduction script. Ensure no breaking changes are introduced to the existing API structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply the fix, run tests, and confirm the crash is resolved'

pro_tips: Always use 'context-first' prompting: Provide your project's architecture file to the AI before asking it to debug a specific function.
Treat the reproduction script as a permanent unit test; if you didn't write a test for it, the bug isn't actually fixed.
Use Claude Code's 'history' feature to audit previous attempts; don't let the AI repeat mistakes you've already ruled out.
When debugging async race conditions, ask the AI to specifically look for shared state access between concurrent promises.
Always perform a 'clean build' after an AI-suggested patch to ensure local cache isn't masking a lingering dependency issue.
resources: Effective Prompt Engineering for Devs: https://anthropic.com/news/claude-code
content.next_steps: Integrate these steps into your standard project boilerplate so every new client project starts with built-in observability.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs derail your freelance reputation. Master these tools, reclaim your development time, and scale your output like a senior engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
